items.prompt: Which source type is measured directly for the present study?
items.explain: Primary data are collected through surveys, experiments, interviews, or observation for the current objective.

items.prompt: In Example 2.3, what problem appears after downloading the census extract?
choices: The file is encrypted and unreadable
Age bins and geographic units do not match the retailer's catchment polygons
The census has no income variables
items.explain: Secondary reuse often shifts effort to re-aggregation when schemas and units do not align with the new question.

items.prompt: A primary survey can yield structured tabular data while a secondary archive can hold unstructured text.
items.explain: Source type and structure are orthogonal: each combination is possible in practice.

items.prompt: Which risk is most associated with real-time streams?
choices: Volume, noise, and integration demands alongside timeliness
Elimination of all sensor calibration needs
items.explain: Real-time sources reward organizations that invest in velocity, quality filtering, and integration infrastructure.

items.prompt: Name one strength and one risk of secondary data.
items.rubric: Strength: speed/scale/historical coverage; risk: mismatch/unknown quality/original-purpose bias.
items.sample_answer: Secondary data are fast and scalable, but schemas may not match the new question and provenance may be incomplete.
